params.description: The 4 boxes pulse to highlight the number of groups.

steps.text: You got the numbers right, but they're switched. The first number is always how many groups. How many boxes do you see?

incorrects.description: Student selected 4 + 2: used addition instead of multiplication

params.description: All boxes pulse to show equal groups structure.

steps.text: You picked addition, but these are equal groups. We use times when every group has the same amount. Which symbol shows groups of the same size?

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 + 4: used addition and reversed the numbers

params.description: All boxes pulse to show equal groups structure.

steps.text: These are equal groups, not parts we're adding. When every group has the same amount, we use times. Which expression uses the times symbol?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System counts the 4 boxes, then counts 2 items in each box, building the expression 4 × 2.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the boxes: 1, 2, 3, 4. That's 4 groups. Now I count what's in one box: 2 items. Equal groups use times, so the expression is 4 times 2. The answer is 4 times 2.
